heavy expense for proper main
tenance.


The distant supply is probably
what the city will have to come to
unless the underground supply
proves to be superior to what even
Its advocates expect. But the un-
derground plan Is the best available
at this time to escape the dire con.
sequences of sewage soon to be
turned into the river at Occur
dAlene, in fact the only speedy re.
course there is.


Meanwhile, If the city secures a
supply that will be good for years
from the subterrean supply source,
or Is forced to reach out for a lake
supply at comparatively early
date, the power station should be
retained.


1t is the city's only source OF
power for a municipal electric light
plant, something that is badly need.
ed now and will be as valuable in
future even though the Graves peo-
pie are competing for city light.
The city plant would be worth its
cost, even II not operated, as
club over both companies.
Naturally both corporations are
keenly interested In taking this last
power site on the river out of the
city's hands, and / or the other
will certainly get it by hook or
crook, If necessary, unless the pub
lie is wide awake to its interests
in retaining the site.


Under direction of the city en-
gineer, rs foot we'll has been sunk
on the up-river site for the purpose
of tapping the underground flow
of water. 1unneling from this
shaft is now being done and a re-
port of It's effectiveness is expect
ed soon. The underground supply
is endorsed by the board Of health,
city engineer and committee of
the 10000 club.


The talk of filtration plant is
believed to be a handy subterfuge
of those who have designs upon
the dam site. Well knowing that
the expense of filtration beds is
prohibitive just now, not to men,
ton the continual expense Of main
tenance and cleaning cost, they be-
lieve that the city may be induced
to give favorable consideration 70
the lake plan Immediately and thus
divert public attention long enough
to accomplish possession Of the
power site.
